title: Static Accessibility Evidence, WCAG Mapping, and Review Handoff · Execution Trace: Structure Language
summary: The crawler counts alt values and headings then declares WCAG compliance without computed names, interactions, contrast, reflow, or disabled-user review. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go structure language increment in WCAG-linked static finding ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Audit document language, headings, landmarks, labels, instructions, table relationships, and duplicate identifiers as evidence, not templates.
Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using surrounding purpose and review uncertainty.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Structure Language · Theory Structure Language
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6436 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to map static image, structure, semantics, interaction risks, uncertainty, and WCAG review boundaries; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Build Structure Language evidence · Theory Structure Language
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-structure-language-gytluo" that uses case 3197 to map static image, structure, semantics, interaction risks, uncertainty, and WCAG review boundaries. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
